signals.headline: Controllers flagging NetSuite flux commentary as #1 manual task in discovery calls
signals.summary: Across 12 discovery calls this week, controllers at Series C–D SaaS companies named 'writing flux commentary into NetSuite' as the single most painful month-end task. Average time spent: 6–9 hours per month per controller. Three prospects asked for a side-by-side demo vs. Numeric.

signals.headline: Lightmatter case: Concourse cut 12+ hours/month from their close
signals.summary: Lightmatter's VP Finance reported on the QBR that Concourse has removed 12+ hours per month of flux narrative writing and reconciling Ramp expense categorizations against their NetSuite chart. They want to co-author a blog post about it.

signals.headline: Three enterprise deals stalled on 'how do you differ from Rogo?' this week
signals.summary: Mid-market → upper mid-market prospects (Maximus, CurbWaste expansion, Pave) all came back with the same blocker: 'We can't tell how Concourse is different from Rogo for the analyst workflow.' Sales is asking for a durable comparison asset.

signals.headline: FP&A leads asking how to safely expose AI agents to board-level numbers
signals.summary: Six prospects this week independently surfaced governance concerns: 'how do we stop the agent from touching unreleased board metrics?' This is a new pattern — three months ago the objection was accuracy, now it's permissions and trust boundaries.

signals.headline: CurbWaste doubled Concourse seats after Q1 — wants a joint case study on 75% manual cut
signals.summary: CurbWaste's CFO confirmed they cut manual reporting work by 75% in Q1 and have budget approved to double seats. Marketing has been asked to turn this into a flagship case study with numbers by end of month.

signals.headline: CFOs at pre-IPO companies asking about audit trail and SOX compliance
signals.summary: Every pre-IPO prospect in the last 10 days asked some version of: 'how will my auditor accept agent-generated work?' Internal audit partners are blocking deals until there's a public, authoritative answer.
